step.text: Apply for Section 8 licence to Regional Director
step.name: Step 1

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. It can earn revenue, but all profits must be applied towards the company's objects (charitable purposes). No dividend distribution to members.
Section 12A: income of the company exempt from tax. Section 80G: donors get tax deduction on donations. Both require separate applications to the Income Tax department.

Non-profit company under Section 8 of Companies Act. Established for promoting commerce, art, science, education, research, social welfare, religion, charity, or protection of environment. Cannot distribute dividends. Profits applied to objectives.

mainEntity.name: What licence is needed for Section 8?

acceptedAnswer.text: Licence from Central Government (Regional Director) under Section 8(1). Application includes: MOA, AOA, draft declaration, estimated income/expenditure for 3 years. Takes 30-60 days for licence. Then normal incorporation.

mainEntity.name: How is Section 8 different from Trust or Society?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 8: governed by Companies Act (higher compliance, more credibility). Trust: governed by Indian Trusts Act (simpler, but less transparent). Society: governed by Societies Registration Act (for membership-based organisations). Section 8 preferred by institutional donors and FCRA.

mainEntity.name: Can Section 8 company receive foreign donations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with FCRA registration from Ministry of Home Affairs. Section 8 companies are preferred recipients for FCRA because of higher transparency and mandatory audits under Companies Act.

mainEntity.name: What are the tax benefits for Section 8 company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Exempt from income tax under Section 12A/12AB if registered with CIT. Donors get Section 80G deduction. No stamp duty (many states). No minimum capital requirement. Professional tax exemption in most states.
